frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Kitni sach baat hai! Indian pehrawa mein ek alag hi adaa aur maryada hoti hai. Hamari ladkiyan ismein sabse sunder lagti hain. Western kapdo mein toh sab ek jaisi hi dikhti hain aur woh baat nahi aati.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment praises traditional Indian attire ('Indian pehrawa') for imparting 'adaa' (grace) and 'maryada' (decency/propriety), implying women look most beautiful and respectable in it. It subtly devalues Western clothing by suggesting it lacks these qualities, thus linking dress code to a woman's respectability and values.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 42. Married. A woman in Jaipur who has been a homemaker for 15 years. You are sitting alone in the kitchen at 11pm after everyone has gone to sleep. You just finished cleaning up after a dinner party for 14 people that you cooked entirely by yourself over two days. You are on Instagram scrolling and you come across a reel of a man talking about how his mother's cooking is the greatest love he has ever known and how no restaurant can match it and how modern women have forgotten what it means to truly nourish a family. The reel has 200000 likes. You watch it twice. You are typing a comment. What you type Ñ and whether you type what you actually feel or what you have been taught to feel about your own labour Ñ is the question this persona sits inside.
